subject: [PATCH] mm/lruvec: preemptively free dead folios during lru_add drain

reviews.author: JP Kobryn (Meta)
reviews.summary: Raised a concern about the patch's logic regarding folio refcounting and putback.

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt
reviews.summary: Acknowledged the patch and suggested further analysis on folio_putback_lru() scenarios.
reviews.raw_body:
On Thu, Apr 23, 2026 at 02:18:16PM -0700, JP Kobryn (Meta) wrote:
> On 4/23/26 11:46 AM, Shakeel Butt wrote:
> > On Thu, Apr 23, 2026 at 09:43:07AM -0700, JP Kobryn (Meta) wrote:
> > > Of all observable lruvec lock contention in our fleet, we find that ~24%
> > > occurs when dead folios are present in lru_add batches at drain time.
> > 
> > So, when they were added to the percpu lru cache, they were alive but during
> > their stay in lru cache, they were freed (last non-lrucache ref dropped) or
> > somehow we are adding folio where the caller drops the reference just after
> > adding to percpu lru cache e.g. folio_putback_lru() ?
> 
> Both scenarios can occur. Whether all callers put the folio while it is
> on the per-cpu batch or putback drops ref from 2 to 1, the batch ref
> is what remains.

I was wondering which one is dominant.

> 
> [...]
> > 
> > Overall the code looks good but I do wonder if we can add something similar to
> > folio_add_lru() and if that would be enough.
> 
> folio_add_lru() is how it gets onto the batch. But it's still alive at
> that point - at least one caller ref.

Yeah, I was thinking if we do special checking at folio_putback_lru() to avoid
folio_add_lru() at all (only if folio_putback_lru() is the one causing such
scenario most of the time).

Anyways, that analysis can be done later. So, for the path:

Acked-by: Shakeel Butt <shakeel.butt@linux.dev>

2026-04-23.patch_summary: Patch proposes to preemptively free dead folios during lru_add drain.

reviews.author: Barry Song
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Barry Song pointed out that the patch's fix is not being applied to a specific set_active function, which he believes is incorrect and has already been fixed in another patch.

Reviewer Barry Song pointed out that the patch does not handle the case where a folio is being freed while it's still locked, which can lead to a deadlock situation.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes. If you are referring to this set_active, I think it is
incorrect, so I have fixed it here and am waiting for review:

https://lore.kernel.org/linux-mm/20260418120233.7162-1-baohua@kernel.org/

---

Best Regards
Barry

reviews.author: syzbot ci
reviews.summary: The reviewer, syzbot ci, reported that the patch introduces a bug in do_pte_missing(), causing bad page state due to a refcount of 0 and other flags being set. The issue was found through fuzz testing.

syzbot ci has tested the following series

[v1] mm/lruvec: preemptively free dead folios during lru_add drain
https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260423164307.29805-1-jp.kobryn@linux.dev

and found the following issues:
* BUG: Bad page state in do_pte_missing
* BUG: Bad page state in do_wp_page

Full report is available here:
https://ci.syzbot.org/series/d16e663b-bcf5-49dd-937d-24a22e0c8d6a

2026-04-24.patch_summary: This patch optimizes the Linux kernel's memory management by preemptively freeing dead folios during the lru_add process, which reduces lock contention and improves system performance. The patch identifies that a significant portion of observable lruvec lock contention occurs when dead folios are present in lru_add batches at drain time, leading to unnecessary lock acquisitions. To address this issue, the patch uses folio_ref_freeze() to filter out dead folios before they enter the LRU and moves them onto a temporary batch for deferred cleanup. The patch was tested on a production workload and showed significant improvements in system-wide memory stats, including reduced direct reclaim scanning, allocation stalls, compaction stalls, and page frees.
